name: MeshingParameters
summary: Represents settings used for creating a mesh representation of a brep or surface.

constructors.name: MeshingParameters()
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance with default values. Initial values are same as Default.

constructors.name: MeshingParameters(Double)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance with simple values, similar to that of Rhino's meshing slider interface.

params.name: density
params.doc: The density and number of mesh polygons, where 0.0 <= density <= 1.0, where 0 quickly creates coarse meshes, and 1 slowly creates dense meshes.

constructors.name: MeshingParameters(MeshingParameters)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance copying values from source. Initial values are same as Default.

constructors.name: MeshingParameters(Double, Double)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance with simple values, similar to that of Rhino's meshing slider interface.

params.name: density
params.doc: The density and number of mesh polygons, where 0.0 <= density <= 1.0, where 0 quickly creates coarse meshes, and 1 slowly creates dense meshes.

params.name: minimumEdgeLength
params.doc: The minimum allowed mesh edge length.

constructors.name: MeshingParameters(SerializationInfo, StreamingContext)
constructors.summary: Called by BinaryFormatter.Deserialize to create a new MeshParamaters object.

methods.name: CopyFrom
methods.summary: Call this method to copy MeshingParameters from another instance.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

methods.name: DocumentCurrentSetting
methods.summary: Gets the MeshingParameters that are currently set for a document. These are the same settings that are shown in the DocumentProperties "mesh settings" user interface.

methods.name: Equals(MeshingParameters)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified MeshingParameters has the same values as the present MeshingParameters.

returns.doc: true if MeshingParameters has the same values as this; otherwise false.

methods.name: Equals(Object)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified MeshingParameters has the same values as the present MeshingParameters.

returns.doc: true if MeshingParameters has the same values as this; otherwise false.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: FromEncodedString
methods.summary: Converts encoded serialized string into a MeshingParameters

returns.doc: Returns a new MeshingParameters created by decoding and deserializing the string or null if value is invalid.

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.summary: Computes a hash number that represents the current MeshingParameters.

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

params.name: info
params.doc: The SerializationInfo to populate with data.

params.name: context
params.doc: The destination (see StreamingContext) for this serialization.

throws.when: The caller does not have the required permission.

methods.name: ToEncodedString
methods.summary: Returns a encoded string that represents the MeshingParameters.

returns.doc: A encoded string that represents the MeshingParameters.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(MeshingParameters mp1, MeshingParameters mp2)
methods.summary: Determines whether the two MeshingParameters have equal values.

returns.doc: true if all of components of the two MeshingParameters are equal; otherwise false.

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(MeshingParameters mp1, MeshingParameters mp2)
methods.summary: Determines whether the two MeshingParameters do not have equal values.

returns.doc: true if any components of the two MeshingParameters are not equal; otherwise false.

properties.name: ClosedObjectPostProcess
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not to post process non-closed meshes that should be closed. If the Brep being meshed is closed, JaggedSeams = false, and ClosedObjectPostProcess = true, and the resulting mesh is not closed, then a post meshing process is applied to find and close gaps in the mesh. Typically the resulting mesh is not closed because the input Brep has a geometric flaw, like loops in trimming curve.

properties.name: Coarse
properties.summary: Gets mesh creation parameters for coarse meshing. This corresponds with the "Jagged and Faster" default in Rhino.

properties.name: ComputeCurvature
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not surface curvature data will be embedded in the mesh.

properties.name: Default
properties.summary: Gets mesh creation parameters to create the default render mesh. Only use this if you plan on specifying your own custom meshing parameters.

properties.name: DefaultAnalysisMesh
properties.summary: Gets mesh creation parameters to create the default analysis mesh.

properties.name: DoublePrecision
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the mesh should have double precision vertices in addition to the floats.

properties.name: FastRenderMesh
properties.summary: Gets mesh creation parameters to create the a render mesh when meshing speed is preferred over mesh quality.

properties.name: GridAmplification
properties.summary: Gets or sets the grid amplification factor. Values lower than 1.0 will decrease the number of initial quads, values higher than 1.0 will increase the number of initial quads.

properties.name: GridAngle
properties.summary: Gets or sets the maximum allowed angle difference (in radians) for a single sampling quad. The angle pertains to the surface normals.

properties.name: GridAspectRatio
properties.summary: Gets or sets the maximum allowed aspect ratio of sampling quads.

properties.name: GridMaxCount
properties.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of grid quads in the initial sampling grid.

properties.name: GridMinCount
properties.summary: Gets or sets the minimum number of grid quads in the initial sampling grid.

properties.name: JaggedSeams
properties.summary: Gets or sets whether or not the mesh is allowed to have jagged seams. When this flag is set to true, meshes on either side of a Brep Edge will not match up.

properties.name: MaximumEdgeLength
properties.summary: Gets or sets the maximum allowed mesh edge length.

properties.name: Minimal
properties.summary: Gets minimal meshing parameters.

properties.name: MinimumEdgeLength
properties.summary: Gets or sets the minimum allowed mesh edge length.

properties.name: MinimumTolerance
properties.summary: Gets or sets the minimum tolerance.

properties.name: QualityRenderMesh
properties.summary: Gets mesh creation parameters to create the a render mesh when mesh quality is preferred over meshing speed.

properties.name: RefineAngle
properties.summary: Gets or sets the mesh parameter refine angle in radians.

properties.name: RefineAngleInDegrees
properties.summary: Gets or sets the mesh parameter refine angle in degrees.

properties.name: RefineGrid
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the sampling grid can be refined when certain tolerances are not met.

properties.name: RelativeTolerance
properties.summary: Gets or sets the relative tolerance.

properties.name: SimplePlanes
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not planar areas are allowed to be meshed in a simplified manner.

properties.name: Smooth
properties.summary: Gets mesh creation parameters for smooth meshing. This corresponds with the "Smooth and Slower" default in Rhino.

properties.name: TextureRange
properties.summary: Gets or sets how and if textures will be packed.

properties.name: Tolerance
properties.summary: Gets or sets the maximum allowed edge deviation. This tolerance is measured between the center of the mesh edge and the surface.
